data.title: IOI Properties to launch W Residences Marina View for public sale
data.description:
Public sale will open on 25 October. 

IOI Properties Singapore will launch its W Residences Marina View - Singapore, the first hotel-home integrated residene,  for public sale on 25 October.

The public launch will be a limited release of 100 units, out of the planned 683 units, from levels 16 to 20. Out of the 100 units available, 85 will be the one-, two-, and three-bedroom residences, starting from $3,230 per square foot.

Complementing these are 15 homes with 4 and 5 bedrooms in the Signature Collection.

W Residences Marina View residents are promised hotel services such as 24/7 concierge services, valet parking, and fixtures and appliances from V-ZUG, DornBracht, and TOTO, amongst others.

The residences are developed by IOI Properties, managed by Marriott International, and are designed by firm architects61 and interior designer Fady Hachem.

data.title: Soon Hock Enterprise to debut on SGX Mainboard
data.description:
The offering was 9.8 times oversubscribed.

Soon Hock Enterprise Holding Limited is set to debut on the SGX-ST Mainboard following strong demand for its Initial Public Offering (IPO).

The company is offering 21.58 million shares at S$0.58 each.

The offering was 9.8 times oversubscribed, with total interest and application monies reaching approximately $122.6m.

Specifically, the International Offer (18.78 million shares) received indications of interest totaling approximately $95.1m, making it 8.7 times oversubscribed.

The Singapore Public Offer (2.8 millio shares) was even more popular, being 16.9 times oversubscribed based on 1,444 valid applications for 47.33 million shares, totaling about $27.4m.

Maybank Securities and United Overseas Bank Limited are serving as the Joint Issue Managers, Joint Global Coordinators, and Joint Bookrunners for the IPO.

data.title: Q&M Dental eyes Thailand expansion with local dental chain deal
data.description:
The deal values the Thai company at $77.8m.

Q&M Dental Group (Singapore) plans to enter Thailand’s private dental market by acquiring an equity stake in a Thai dental clinic chain, according to a bourse filing.

The deal, outlined in a memorandum of understanding, values the Thai company at $77.8m (฿1.95b) on a cash- and debt-free basis, with final adjustments to be made upon completion.

The unnamed Thai target runs one of the country’s largest privately owned dental networks, with over 30 clinics across Thailand, mainly in Bangkok and the northeastern provinces of Udon Thani, Khon Kaen, and Ubon Ratchathani.

The acquisition would mark Q&M’s first move into Thailand, expanding its presence beyond Singapore and Malaysia.

Following the deal, one of the target company’s founders will sign a 15-year service agreement to remain as CEO or managing director.

The agreement also includes a profit guarantee that starts at $4.9m (฿123m) in 2026, rising to $11.4m (฿285m) by 2030, and remaining at least that level until 2033.

($1 = ฿25.08)

data.title: Jardine Cycle & Carriage appoints new independent chairman of the board
data.description:
Samuel Tsien will be independent chairman of the board, effective 11 November.

Jardine Cycle & Carriage has appointed Samuel Tsien as its independent chairman of the board, effective 11 November.

He is currently a non-executive independent director and was first appointed to the board in October 2021.

Prior to this, he was Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation’s group CEO from 2021 to 2022.

data.title: Gov't to curb F1 disruption on businesses
data.description:
This will include improving access and wayfinding around Marina Bay.

Singapore government agencies will implement measures to reduce disruption on businesses during the F1 Singapore Grand Prix.

This will include improving access and wayfinding around Marina Bay and ensuring malls remain accessible by public transport and hired cars.

The road closure period has been significantly reduced, dropping to seven days today from 12 days in 2008.

Since 2008, the grand prix has attracted over 720,000 international visitors and generated more than $2.2b in incremental tourism receipts.

data.title: OxPay utilises net proceeds from convertible loan of $1.8m
data.description:
It has been used for operating expenses, amongst others.

OxPay Financial Limited has fully utilised net proceeds from convertible loan of $1.8m, after deducting the costs and expenses of approximately $161,000.

It has been used for operating expenses, employee remuneration, repayment of existing loan facilities, and directors’ fees.

The company entered into a loan agreement with Oxley Capital Management last 17 January 2025 to which the lender granted up to $2m.

data.title: 17Live’s independent director Chen Xiuling resigns
data.description:
This followed her inclusion in the US' list of Specially Designated Nationals.

17LIVE Group Limited’s Independent Director, Chen Xiuling, has voluntarily resigned as director on 15 October.

This comes after she was included in the Specially Designated Nationals List by the US Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control, which includes individuals with whom US persons are prohibited from doing business.

The company said Xiuling has engaged with 17LIVE Group in her oversight capacity only, and is not involved in the company's daily business and operations.

Further, 17LIVE has never done business with Xiuling, the company she works in, DW Capital or the shareholder of DW Capital, Chen Zhi.

data.title: SGX launches SDR linkage mirroring Indonesian blue chips
data.description:
Singaporean investors can gain easy access to Indonesian-listed securities by using their local brokers.

The Singapore Exchange has launched the Singapore Depository Receipt (SDR) Linkage mirroring renowned Indonesian listed blue chips.

Issued by Phillip Securities, the Indonesia SDRs represent beneficial ownership interests in the underlying IDX-listed companies of Bank Central Asia, Telkom Indonesia and Indofood CBP.

Through this, Singaporean investors can gain easy access to Indonesian-listed securities by using their local brokers during SGX trading hours.

The launch coincides with the Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) trading at an all-time high in September 2025, having delivered an annualised return of over 9% (a 150% total return) over the past decade.

data.title: SIA Group lifts passenger traffic in September with 8% growth
data.description:
The group carried 3.38 million passengers during the month, an 8.0% increase compared to the same period last year.

Singapore Airlines (SIA) Group reported continued growth in passenger traffic in September 2025, with total traffic rising 3.7% YoY, outpacing a 2.5% increase in capacity.

As a result, the group’s overall passenger load factor (PLF) rose by 1.0 percentage point to 87.1%. The group carried 3.38 million passengers during the month, an 8.0% increase compared to the same period last year.

Singapore Airlines’ mainline operations recorded a PLF of 86.2%, whilst low-cost subsidiary Scoot posted a stronger PLF of 90.1%.

For Singapore Airlines, available seat-kilometres (ASK) rose by 1.0%, whilst revenue passenger-kilometres (RPK) grew 1.5%. Passenger numbers were up 4.4% year-on-year, helping lift the PLF by 0.5 percentage points to 86.2%.

Regionally, the strongest PLF improvements were seen in East Asia (up 3.5 points to 83.7%) and West Asia & Africa (up 2.0 points to 82.3%). However, declines were recorded in the Americas (−2.1 points to 84.2%), Europe (−1.6 points to 88.9%), and the Southwest Pacific (−0.8 point to 90.5%).

Scoot recorded more robust growth, with ASK up 8.3% and RPK up 11.8%, leading to a 15.5% rise in passenger volumes. Its PLF rose by 2.8 percentage points to 90.1%.

PLF gains were led by East Asia (up 4.3 points to 89.1%), whilst West Asia and the Rest of World regions both saw slight declines of 0.4 percentage points, to 87.7% and 94.0% respectively.

On the cargo front, group-wide capacity contracted by 0.8%, whilst cargo loads fell 3.8%, resulting in a 1.8 percentage-point decline in the cargo load factor to 56.2%.

SIA Group attributed the lower cargo performance partly to Typhoon Ragasa, which disrupted East Asia freighter operations.

Despite the dip in load factor, total cargo and mail carried rose slightly by 1.0% to 94.1 million kilograms. All reported regions saw lower cargo load factors, with the largest drop in West Asia & Africa (down 3.0 points).

As of end-September, the group’s passenger network spanned 129 destinations across 37 countries and territories, with Singapore Airlines operating to 78 destinations and Scoot to 73. The cargo network reached 133 destinations in 38 countries and territories. These counts include Singapore as a destination.

data.title: Propnex calls October BTO launch largest since 2022 with 9,144 flats
data.description:
PropNex expects overall application rates to range between 3.0 and 3.5 times. 

PropNex has described HDB’s October 2025 Build-To-Order (BTO) launch as the largest since November 2022, noting the offering of 9,144 flats across 10 projects in eight towns.

With this launch, 2025 has seen a total of 19,723 BTO flats (29,975 including Sale of Balance Flats), placing supply well above typical levels for a single year.

The mix of flats in this exercise includes four Prime projects, one Plus, and five Standard.

Based on PropNex’s estimates, this corresponds to 41% of units classified as Prime, 16% as Plus, and 43% as Standard, reflecting a continued diversification of public housing under the new classification model introduced by HDB.

Two headline projects are expected to attract significant attention. Mount Pleasant Crest in Toa Payoh will offer 1,348 flats near the future Mount Pleasant MRT station.

Located in the Core Central Region, the project includes a “white flat” layout option, open-concept units without internal partition walls.

Meanwhile, Berlayar Residences in Bukit Merah, part of the Greater Southern Waterfront, offers 880 flats close to Telok Blangah MRT and the Central Business District.

PropNex noted the last BTO in the area, launched in May 2021, saw an application rate of 23 times. Berlayar’s Prime classification includes a 14% subsidy recovery upon resale after the Minimum Occupation Period.

PropNex expects overall application rates to range between 3.0 and 3.5 times, with heightened competition for larger units.

Just four of the 10 projects offer 5-room flats, totaling 1,002 units in Bedok, Jurong East, Sengkang, and Yishun. These are likely to draw strong interest from second-timer families.

This launch also sees the rollout of the Family Care Scheme’s Joint Balloting feature, which allows parents and children—regardless of marital status—to apply together for two flats in the same BTO project where 2-room Flexi or 3-room flats are available.

Singles, too, will see expanded access, with 2,923 units of 2-room Flexi flats—the highest such supply in a single launch since at least February 2019. PropNex pointed out that the July 2025 exercise saw 5.6 times the subscription for this flat type.

Shorter Waiting Time (SWT) flats, with waiting periods under three years, are another draw. These make up 3,294 flats—or 36% of total supply—across four projects, and are likely to appeal to buyers seeking faster occupancy.

Looking ahead, the next BTO launch in February 2026 will offer approximately 4,600 flats across six projects in Bukit Merah, Sembawang, Tampines, and Toa Payoh.
